name: Cream of Vegetable Soup
recipeInstructions:
Use one or a combination of: asparagus, broccoli, carrots, cauliflower, celery, mushrooms, onions, string beans. Cook vegetables, drain well and chop into small pieces. Use within 30 minutes or refrigerate them.
Place broth in a saucepan and bring to a simmer over moderate heat.
In a small bowl, mix flour, dry milk and melted margarine until it forms coarse crumbs.
Stir the flour mixture into the simmering broth using a wire whip.
OR Stir the flour mixture into cold broth, cook and stir until smooth. Cook until the starchy taste is gone. Add chopped vegetables to the sauce and reheat to serving temperature. Taste for seasoning and add salt and pepper.
Nutritive values are based on the use of commercial broth with no added salt. Serve hot. Makes 4 cups.
1 cup serving - 121 calories, 1 skim milk, 1 fat exchange; 12 grams carbohydrate, 5 grams protein, 6 grams fat, 1099 mg sodium.
For low-sodium diets: Use fresh or frozen vegetables cooked without salt, low-sodium broth and salt-free margarine. Do not add salt.
For low-cholesterol diets: May be used as written.
